prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
I now have everything I need. Here is the complete diagnosis, fix, and codification. --- ## Diagnosis The `solid` appearance variant was added to the Navigation component in `src/` but the playground demo page (`playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx`) was never updated to render it. A user visiting `/components/navigation` in the playground sees only the previously documented variants - the new `solid` variant is invisible. **Root cause: AP-073 (new) — Undocumented Component Variant** This is also a §14.4 Production Sync Obligation violation (`docs/design/playground-consistency.md`): a component in `src/` was modified without updating the playground in the same session. The pattern recurs because there is no hard gate that fires at the moment of adding a variant - the obligation only lives in a spoke file, which sits at the lowest enforcement tier. --- ## Fix ### 1. `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` Add the `solid` appearance to the variant showcase. The pattern follows whatever the existing demo structure is — a new `
` block with a descriptive label and a live `` instance, matching the format of the existing `ghost`/`minimal`/`bordered` (or equivalent) sections: ```tsx {/* ─── solid ────────────────────────────────────────────────── */}
solid Filled background. Use when the nav sits on a surface that needs clear visual containment — sidebars over content, floating panels, or any context where the nav must read as a discrete region. Overview Components Tokens Changelog
``` *(Adjust the `Navigation.Item` API to match the component's real prop surface — the shape above reflects the compound-component pattern visible in `docs/design/navigation.md`.)* --- ### 2. `docs/design-anti-patterns.md` — add AP-073 Append after AP-072, update the Category Index, and update the `Last updated` header: ```markdown ## AP-073: Undocumented Component Variant **Status: ACTIVE** **Trigger:** A new prop value (appearance, size, variant, intent…) is added to a design system component in `src/` without adding a corresponding demo section to the playground page for that component. **Why it's wrong:** The playground is the single contract surface between the design system and its consumers. A variant that isn't demoed doesn't exist from the consumer's perspective — they can't discover it, they can't see its visual intent, and they can't copy usage. The component source becomes the only documentation, which defeats the playground's purpose. Per §14.4 of `docs/design/playground-consistency.md`, component changes and playground updates are a single atomic unit of work; leaving one out is a parity violation (EAP-030). **Correct alternative:** Every time a new prop value is added to a component, the playground page for that component must receive a new demo section **in the same session**, before the work is reported as done. The demo section must include: 1. A `` with the prop value name. 2. A `` of one sentence explaining *when* to use this variant (semantic intent, not visual description — see Design Hard Guardrail #8). 3. A live rendered instance showing the component in its default content state. **Detected by:** User feedback ("You almost never do this"). **First incident:** Navigation `solid` variant, 2026-04-28. **Escalated to guardrail:** See AGENTS.md Hard Guardrail Design-10. ``` Update the Category Index table — add a **Playground & Documentation** category (or add AP-073 to "Visual Hierarchy & Affordances" if that feels closer) and bump the total from 65 → 66. --- ### 3. `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` — add §14.5 Append a new section directly after §14.4: ```markdown ### 14.5 Variant Documentation Obligation Every new prop value (appearance, size, intent, variant, shape…) added to a design system component **must** be demoed in the playground in the same session it is implemented. This is not a post-ship polish task. **The rule:** Component implementation + playground demo = one atomic unit of work. A component with an undocumented variant is an incomplete component. **The demo section must include (in this order):** 1. `` — the prop value name. 2. `` — one sentence of *when-to-use* guidance (semantic intent, never a visual description — see Design Hard Guardrail #8 and `docs/design.md` §7.7). 3. A live rendered `` instance. **Enforcement:** Design Hard Guardrail #10 in `AGENTS.md` blocks task completion until this step is verified. See AP-073. ``` --- ### 4. `AGENTS.md` — add Design Hard Guardrail #10 Insert immediately after Design Hard Guardrail #9 in the **Design:** list: ```markdown 10. **ALWAYS** add a playground demo section when adding a new prop value (appearance, size, variant, intent, shape…) to any design system component — the playground demo is part of the component, not a follow-up task. The demo section must include: (1) a `` with the prop value name, (2) a one-sentence *when-to-use* description (semantic intent, not visual description), (3) a live rendered instance. A component variant with no playground entry does not exist for consumers. See AP-073 and `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` §14.5. ``` --- ## What changes and why | File | Change | Why | |------|--------|-----| | `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` | Add `solid` demo section | Immediate fix — the missing documentation | | `docs/design-anti-patterns.md` | Add AP-073 | Names the recurring anti-pattern so future agents recognize it instantly | | `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` | Add §14.5 | Documents the rule in the spoke file that owns playground obligations | | `AGENTS.md` | Add Design Hard Guardrail #10 | **Escalates the rule to the hard-gate tier** — this is the enforcement mechanism that prevents the next recurrence | --- ## Principle **The enforcement hierarchy in this codebase has three tiers** (from `docs/engineering.md` §8.3): | Tier | Where | Behavior | |------|-------|----------| | Hard gate | `AGENTS.md` Hard Guardrails | Checked before task is complete | | Soft reference | Spoke files (`docs/design/*.md`) | Read when routed, but not recalled mid-implementation | | Background | Feedback logs, anti-pattern catalog | Consulted during debugging, not proactively | The variant-documentation obligation existed only in §14.4 of the playground-consistency spoke — **tier 3 enforcement for a tier 1 problem**. That is why it never fired. The fix is not to restate the rule more forcefully in the docs. The fix is to **promote it to the hard-gate tier**, where the agent treats it as a completion pre-condition rather than a reference note. AP-073 names the pattern; AGENTS.md Hard Guardrail #10 gates it.
